bible:
IDENTITY
- Title: Shattered Horizon
- Pitch: A disgraced engineer watches her greatest rival destroy the orbital colony she secretly built
- Setting: Corio Station, the sole deep-space mining hub of the 2180 Outer Belt Authority

PROTAGONIST
- Name: Linnea Voss
- Public face: Chief Structural Integrity Engineer of Corio Station, a workaholic with a 4.2 approval rating from the crew
- Hidden truth: The actual architect of Corio Station and every operational blueprint for the last seven years
- The wound: Her public credit for the station’s design was erased years ago—her name scrubbed from every file—by the same man who now claims her inventions as his own

ANTAGONIST
- Name: Silvano Rourke, Director of the Outer Belt Authority
- Why they feel justified: He believes the colony would collapse without his iron hand and that the ends justify stealing a woman’s legacy to maintain order
- What they're protecting or pursuing: Absolute control over Corio’s mineral exports to secure his political future back on Earth

ENABLER
- Name: Elias "Grit" Marr, Lead Maintenance Supervisor
- Their relationship to the protagonist: Longtime friend and surrogate brother, the only person who knows part of the truth about Linnea’s designs
- Why they fail the protagonist: He withheld critical evidence about the station’s vulnerability under pressure, fearing panic would derail the colony’s productivity targets

CATALYST
- Name: Dr. Nia Tarkov, Head of the Outer Belt Authority Research Division
- Why they're dangerous: She has uncovered anomalous power fluctuations on Corio—fluctuations only someone familiar with Linnea’s original non-standard energy grid could explain—and she’s about to present her findings to Rourke

WORLD
- Institution: The Outer Belt Authority, a corporate-government hybrid overseeing all mineral extraction in the Jovian system
- Key locations: Corio Hub (the central spire), The Deep Cut (the lower habitation ring prone to structural stress), The Vault (the secure archive where blueprints are stored), The Edge (a derelict refinery now used for covert meetings)
- What everyone assumes about the power structure: That the Outer Belt Authority and its director, Silvano Rourke, are the sole architects of Corio Station’s success

STAKES
- What the protagonist secretly holds: A full set of original, encrypted schematics proving her authorship of Corio Station
- What's being taken from them: Her professional identity and rightful claim to the station’s design patents
- The tangible proof: A single encrypted data chip hidden behind a maintenance panel in The Deep Cut, accessible only via Linnea’s retinal key and a voiceprint
- The ticking clock: Corio Station’s next structural integrity audit in 47 days—after which, any flaws uncovered will be blamed on its “original, flawed designer”

DRAMATIC IRONY
- What the reader knows from chapter one: Linnea secretly funds Corio Station’s operational shortfalls through an anonymous benefactor’s account
- What the antagonist won't learn until too late: That the “benefactor” is Linnea herself

LOVE INTEREST
- Name: Captain Jace Duvall, Corio Station Security Oversight
- Their connection to the conflict: He was tasked by Rourke to investigate structural anomalies—an investigation that will expose Linnea’s hidden influence
- What keeps them apart: Jace reports to Rourke; Linnea believes he’s complicit in her erasure
- What will bring them together: Jace discovers Linnea’s hidden schematics during a safety drill—and realizes she’s the one who’s been silently saving lives all along
beats:
CHAPTER 1: The Mask
Summary: Linnea Voss oversees a routine structural integrity scan in The Deep Cut, her competence masking her quiet fury as she catches a misaligned support strut—then corrects it herself before anyone notices, while Elias "Grit" Marr watches but says nothing.
Emotional temperature: unease
Characters present: Linnea Voss, Elias "Grit" Marr
Cliffhanger: Grit pockets a cracked data slate Linnea uses to log the fix, unaware it contains her encrypted schematics.
Dramatic irony: The reader knows Linnea secretly funnels her own funds into Corio’s shortfalls, but no one else does.

---

CHAPTER 2: The Strike
Summary: At the weekly OBA briefing in The Vault, Silvano Rourke publicly rebukes Linnea for "negligence" in the recent structural scan, stripping her of oversight duties in front of directors, engineers, and Captain Jace Duvall—while projecting her corrected schematics on the wall as "flawed design protocols."
Emotional temperature: humiliation
Characters present: Linnea Voss, Silvano Rourke, Elias "Grit" Marr, Captain Jace Duvall, Dr. Nia Tarkov
Cliffhanger: Jace, tasked with investigating the anomaly, locks eyes with Linnea for a second too long—then looks away.
Dramatic irony: The audience sees Linnea’s hands clenched under the table, hiding the data slate Grit stole.

---

CHAPTER 3: The Silence
Summary: Alone in her quarters, Linnea replays the briefing, her reflection in the viewport sharpening into calculation; afterward, she meets Grit in The Edge, where he finally admits he kept her data slate—and that he fears what Rourke would do if he knew the truth.
Emotional temperature: tension
Characters present: Linnea Voss, Elias "Grit" Marr
Cliffhanger: Grit leaves the slate on a rusted pipe in The Edge, but Linnea doesn’t retrieve it—she walks away instead.
Dramatic irony: The reader knows Linnea never intended to take it back—she’s already planning something else.

---

CHAPTER 4: The Strut
Summary: From Rourke’s POV, the chapter shows him hosting a live feed from Corio’s surface to Earth investors, boasting about “his” orbital marvel while privately doubting his own blueprints after a minor life-support fluctuation in The Deep Cut.
Emotional temperature: satisfaction
Characters present: Silvano Rourke, Dr. Nia Tarkov, Outer Belt Authority directors
Cliffhanger: Tarkov presents her findings to Rourke—anomalous power spikes matching Linnea’s original non-standard energy grid—but Rourke dismisses her as “overcautious.”
Dramatic irony: The reader knows Tarkov’s discovery is exactly what could prove Linnea’s authorship—if Rourke understood it.

---

CHAPTER 5: The Overreach
Summary: Rourke attempts to reboot a failed atmospheric filter array in The Deep Cut using Linnea’s old protocols—only for the system to overload, causing a localized pressure breach that Grit barely seals before evacuation is required.
Emotional temperature: unease
Characters present: Silvano Rourke, Elias "Grit" Marr, maintenance crew
Cliffhanger: Grit mutters to a coworker, “This never happened under Linnea’s watch,” as Rourke storms off.
Dramatic irony: The reader knows Linnea’s non-standard grid was designed to prevent exactly this type of cascade failure.

---

CHAPTER 6: The Quiet Move
Summary: Linnea anonymously reroutes OBA funds to cover a crew bonus delay—using an untraceable account—and does it during a public address by Rourke in The Vault, so the sudden deposits hit accounts mid-speech.
Emotional temperature: tension
Characters present: Linnea Voss (hidden), Silvano Rourke, Outer Belt Authority directors
Cliffhanger: Rourke’s face flickers with confusion as his executive terminal pings with “unauthorized allocation” alerts.
Dramatic irony: The reader knows Linnea used her own hidden fortune to stabilize morale—exactly what Rourke fears most: chaos from below.

---

CHAPTER 7: The Wobble
Summary: A mid-shift safety drill in The Deep Cut fails when a supposedly reinforced bulkhead cracks under pressure—something no one expected after Rourke’s “improvements.”
Emotional temperature: unease
Characters present: Elias "Grit" Marr, maintenance crew, Captain Jace Duvall (observer)
Cliffhanger: One worker turns to Grit and asks, “Was this designed like this?”—using Linnea’s name.
Dramatic irony: The reader knows Linnea’s original specs called for stronger alloys in that exact spot.

---

CHAPTER 8: The Bleed
Summary: Three crises unfold at once: Rourke’s political ally on Earth withdraws support after learning of the unauthorized fund reroute; Tarkov’s research is leaked to an OBA faction questioning Rourke’s competence; and a coolant leak in The Vault forces evacuation of archives—where the encrypted schematics are stored.
Emotional temperature: tension
Characters present: Silvano Rourke, Dr. Nia Tarkov, Outer Belt Authority directors, Captain Jace Duvall
Cliffhanger: Rourke receives a priority alert: someone accessed the Vault archive—the retinal scan was Linnea’s.
Dramatic irony: The reader knows Linnea retrieved her own schematics years ago and hid them elsewhere.

---

CHAPTER 9: The Offer
Summary: Rourke corners Linnea in the corridor outside The Vault and offers her a full pardon and restored title—if she returns to active service and validates his design changes.
Emotional temperature: triumph (for Linnea, unease for Rourke)
Characters present: Linnea Voss, Silvano Rourke
Cliffhanger: Linnea says, “Too late,” and walks away as Rourke’s comms suddenly buzz with news that Tarkov has been detained.
Dramatic irony: The reader knows Linnea has no intention of returning—and that Tarkov’s detention is the first step in Rourke’s unraveling.

---

CHAPTER 10: The Scope
Summary: The reader sees Linnea’s hidden ledger: she has secretly funded Corio’s survival for seven years through shell corporations, fixed structural flaws before they failed, and even planted false data to protect the station—all while Rourke took credit.
Emotional temperature: triumph
Characters present: Linnea Voss (alone, accessing data)
Cliffhanger: A comm ping from Jace interrupts her—he’s outside her quarters.
Dramatic irony: The reader now understands Linnea isn’t just seeking revenge—she’s been dismantling Rourke’s empire from within for years.

---

CHAPTER 11: The Freefall
Summary: Rourke’s chief aide resigns after discovering Rourke falsified audit reports; Tarkov is released but branded an “alarmist”; and Jace, now investigating structural anomalies, stumbles upon Linnea’s hidden data slate in The Deep Cut—its retinal lock reading her name in real time.
Emotional temperature: tension
Characters present: Silvano Rourke, Dr. Nia Tarkov, Captain Jace Duvall
Cliffhanger: Jace activates the slate—only for the screen to display Linnea’s face and the words: “You’ve been looking for me.”
Dramatic irony: Rourke still has no idea Linnea is the architect—he thinks the slate is a relic of an earlier engineer.

---

CHAPTER 12: The Reveal
Summary: Linnea confronts Rourke in the spire’s observation deck at sunset, projecting every flaw in Corio’s design onto the viewport—exposing how his “improvements” nearly killed hundreds.
Emotional temperature: triumph
Characters present: Linnea Voss, Silvano Rourke
Cliffhanger: Rourke’s comm bursts with a live feed from Earth—his resignation letter has been auto-signed and submitted.
Dramatic irony: The reader knows Linnea orchestrated the resignation by leaking evidence to Earth’s oversight board days earlier.

---

CHAPTER 13: The Mirror
Summary: Rourke, now a disgraced shell of himself, is escorted from Corio Station under armed guard, while Linnea watches from the same deck—only for Jace to appear behind her, holding the now-fully decrypted schematics.
Emotional temperature: unease
Characters present: Linnea Voss, Captain Jace Duvall, Silvano Rourke (departing)
Cliffhanger: Jace says, “I found something else in the data… you funded this place. For years.”
Dramatic irony: The reader knows this revelation forces Linnea to confront her own hidden generosity—and Jace’s new role in her life.

---
CHAPTER 14: The Terms
Summary: Linnea meets with the new OBA interim director and negotiates a full public pardon, restoration of her patents, and a seat on the design review board—but only if Rourke is allowed to live in civilian exile.
Emotional temperature: triumph
Characters present: Linnea Voss, new OBA director, Captain Jace Duvall
Cliffhanger: As they leave the meeting, Jace asks, “What about the love interest?” and Linnea smiles for the first time in years.
Dramatic irony: The reader knows Linnea has finally chosen connection over isolation.

---
CHAPTER 15: The Throne
Summary: Corio Station holds a public memorial for its “unsung architect,” with Linnea’s name etched into the central spire alongside Rourke’s—now crossed out—while she and Jace walk the lower habitation ring, now reinforced with her original specs.
Emotional temperature: triumph
Characters present: Linnea Voss, Captain Jace Duvall, crew of Corio Station
Cliffhanger: Linnea pauses at the same cracked data slate from Chapter 1—now welded shut—and Jace picks it up, asking, “What do we do with this?” She replies, “Let it rust.”
Dramatic irony: The reader knows the slate holds the final proof of Linnea’s genius—and her mercy.
summaries: Linnea concealed anomalies in strut repairs, Grit exposed her secret logs, and Director Rourke summoned a crisis meeting, escalating tensions between them.
Linnea's unauthorized structural fixes were exposed by Rourke, stripping her of oversight and shifting power to Marr, while Jace subtly challenged her to keep fighting.
Linnea and Grit's tense confrontation revealed Rourke's hidden motives and Linnea's plan to expose him, shifting their alliance and setting a dangerous course for Corio Station.
Silvano Rourke silenced Nia Tarkov's warnings about Corio Station's structural flaws, but a sudden atmospheric failure forces him to investigate the original, hidden grid design.
Rourke ordered Grit to fix the failing filters, revealing Linnea’s hidden legacy protocol, but unauthorized access in The Vault forced Rourke to pursue her, exposing her return.
Linnea exposed Rourke's corruption, triggered a station-wide alert, and revealed his structural sabotage, while Jace confirmed her suspicions about the failing node.
Linnea and Jace uncover Rourke's sabotage of the station's alloy skins, causing fractures during a drill, revealing Linnea's hidden knowledge and design.
Silvano Rourke learns Linnea Voss accessed The Vault, stole her schematics, and fled as the station collapses around her and Tarkov's data exposes Corio's flaws.
Linnea rejected Silvano Rourke's offer to restore her title, exposing his sabotage of the station and his detention of Dr. Tarkov for knowing too much.

Linnea accessed hidden data exposing Rourke's corruption, confronted Jace about his allegiances, and discovered a secret message revealing he was the one who built the bypass she used, shifting their dynamic and uncovering a deeper conspiracy.
Rourke's control over Corio Station crumbles as Linnea exposes his sabotage, aided by Nia and Jace, leading to his resignation and her revelation of the truth.
Rourke's control over Corio Station crumbles as Linnea exposes his sabotage, aided by Nia and Jace, leading to his forced resignation.
Jace uncovered Linnea secretly funded Corio Station, switching from suspect to protector as he defied Rourke to escort her into protective custody.
Linnea secured pardons, patents, and a seat on the review board for herself and exile for Rourke by exposing his sabotage, while Jace supported her claims and their relationship subtly shifted.
Linnea and Jace confront Rourke's erased legacy and preserve his hidden truth by welding shut his incriminating data slate.
tropeDisplay: secret-benefactor + cast-out + let-them-fail + enemy-to-lover [space station, orbital mining colony, 2180]
